Hi Muhammad,

Thank you for applying to the Field Coordinator role with our Impact & Policy team. We wanted to reach out personally because we genuinely reviewed your profile, and we appreciate the time you invested in putting together your application.

we're going to be direct with you, because we think you deserve honesty rather than a generic rejection, and we hope this feedback is useful as you think about your next move.

The role we're hiring for sits at a pretty specific intersection of skills. We need someone who can walk into a government school in rural Islamabad or a district in Punjab, observe an enumerator conducting a survey, and immediately catch if they're deviating from our sampling plan or cutting corners on consent procedures. We need them to flag data quality issues in real time, push back when external survey firms aren't meeting our standards, and manage the relationship with those vendors firmly but professionally. It's a role that demands hands-on experience supervising fieldworkers and deep familiarity with how research data collection actually works on the ground.

Looking at your background, we can see you have solid community mobilization experience from your USAID work. That's genuinely valuable, community trust and stakeholder relationships are real skills. We also see you've listed Kobo and ODK in your technical skills, which tells me you're aware

systems-driven experience I have primarily developed over the years. I

large-scale programs, stakeholder coordination, and monitoring & evaluation
systems. In this regard, I would like to highlight that during my tenure at
PIMS under the Sehat Sahulat Program, I led a multidisciplinary team of
over 300 personnel, including doctors, pharmacists, dispensers, computer
operators, LDCs, and support staff. Additionally, across my professional
career, I have gained extensive experience in sales & marketing,
management, administration, operations, procurement, human resource
management, project management, compliance, and reporting. This diverse
exposure has significantly strengthened my leadership, operational
oversight, and cross-functional coordination capabilities within complex
environments.

Your feedback has been insightful and will certainly guide me in further
aligning my profile with roles that require more direct field engagement
and survey-based operations, should I choose to expand in that direction.

I remain very interested in staying connected with Taleemabad and would
welcome the opportunity to be considered for future roles within the Impact
& Policy or program management domains, where my background in operations,
implementation, and M&E system design can contribute meaningfully.
